rationale: Current title is Project Manager at Initech (a fictional company/honeypot signal), career history shows business analyst, sales, mechanical engineering, and graphic designer roles with no ML/AI engineering depth, and despite listing 'Data Science' as a skill, the actual work descriptions are entirely non-technical consulting and operations work with zero evidence of production IR or ranking systems.

Only 2.7 years of experience at a single IT-services firm (Tech Mahindra) doing frontend engineering, with AI exposure limited to a small RAG side project and online courses, placing this well below the production ML/IR depth required for a founding-team Senior AI Engineer role.

Career history is entirely Sales Executive and Business Analyst roles at manufacturing companies including Stark Industries (a fictional/honeypot company), with no engineering, ML, or AI production experience whatsoever despite AI-curiosity language in the summary.

Strong data engineering and MLOps background with TensorFlow, Kubeflow, and Spark at a mix of product companies and TCS, but the profile shows analytics/pipeline infrastructure work rather than production IR, retrieval, or ranking systems, and the stated goal of 'doing more ML itself' signals a transition rather than established expertise in embeddings/hybrid search/NDCG optimization.

Content Writer and Customer Support roles at Stark Industries and Dunder Mifflin (both fictional/honeypot companies), with no engineering or ML background; the technical skills listed (Data Pipelines, REST APIs, SQL) are beginner/intermediate with minimal duration and inconsistent with a non-technical career history.

Career spans Graphic Designer, HR Manager, Marketing Manager, and Project Manager roles at Initech (fictional company) and TCS with no ML or software engineering work; skills listed are entirely beginner-level with 3-7 month durations indicating keyword inflation rather than real expertise.

Civil Engineer and Operations Manager background at Infosys and Initech (fictional company) with no ML, AI, or software engineering experience; beginner-level cloud skills (GCP, AWS) with under 17 months duration do not constitute relevant technical depth for a Senior AI Engineer role.

Career consists entirely of Mechanical Engineer, HR Manager, and Accountant roles at Wayne Enterprises (a fictional/honeypot company) and manufacturing companies, with no ML, AI, or software engineering background and skills like Six Sigma and SQL that are irrelevant to production ranking systems.

Accountant, HR Manager, and Customer Support roles at Initech (fictional company) and Globex with no engineering or ML production work; despite a B.E. in Artificial Intelligence from Jadavpur University (tier_2), the actual career shows zero application of that education in technical roles.

Marketing Manager at Hooli with prior Accountant and Content Writer roles at TCS and Stark Industries (fictional company), representing a non-technical profile with no engineering or ML work; the AI curiosity in the summary and beginner gRPC/CI-CD skills do not offset a career with zero production ML experience.
